title: Couch to 5K (Evidence-Based)
description: The NHS Couch to 5K plan: a 9-week progressive run-walk program shown in peer-reviewed trials to take inactive adults to a continuous 5K run. Built on progressive overload and adequate recovery (Daniels' Running Formula).
resources: Daniels, J. (2013). Daniels' Running Formula, 3rd ed.
Buchheit, M. & Laursen, P. (2013). HIIT — solutions to the programming puzzle.
advice: Three runs per week with a full rest day between. If you feel persistent soreness, repeat the previous week before progressing — supercompensation needs full recovery.

subGoals.title: Week 3: Run 3 min continuously
subGoals.description: Aerobic base forming. Capillary density increases ~3-4 weeks in.

subGoals.title: Week 5: Run 20 min continuously
subGoals.description: First major aerobic milestone. Mitochondrial adaptations measurable.

subGoals.title: Week 9: Run 5K (30 min) continuously
subGoals.description: Target distance. Pace is conversational — talk-test passable.

tasks.title: Run day 1 (alternating intervals)
tasks.description: Follow week's prescribed run/walk intervals. 5-min warm-up walk, then intervals, then 5-min cool-down.

tasks.title: Run day 2
tasks.description: Same prescription as day 1. Full rest day between.

tasks.title: Strength & mobility (20 min)
tasks.description: Bodyweight squats, calf raises, glute bridges — reduces running injury risk ~50% (Lauersen et al., 2014, Br J Sports Med).

tasks.title: Sleep ≥7h on run nights
tasks.description: Sleep restriction below 7h doubles injury risk in athletes (Milewski et al., 2014).
